ingredients.name: Tsukasakigyokabuishikawanomieigyosho
ingredients.localName: 司企業（株）石川能美営業所
ingredients.address: Tsukasakigyokabuishikawanomieigyosho, U-200番 Aomachi, Nomi, Ishikawa 923-1101, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This logistics office in Nomi, Ishikawa serves as a background location in Episode 6 as Aoteru's forces mobilize through Seii territory, capturing the everyday infrastructure of the wartime Seii nation.

ingredients.name: Komatsu Airport
ingredients.address: Komatsu Airport, ５０番地先 Yo Ukiyanagimachi, Komatsu, Ishikawa 923-0993, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Komatsu Airport appears in Episode 6 as a key transit point for Seii military movements, its runways and terminal standing in for the contested northern gateway that Aoteru's alliance must secure.

ingredients.name: Tani Tunnel
ingredients.address: Tani Tunnel, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Tani Tunnel features in Episode 6 as a strategic passage through the Fukui highlands, used during troop movements that precede the campaign into Seii's heartland.

ingredients.name: Fukui Prefectural Government Office
ingredients.address: Fukui Prefectural Government Office, 3 Chome-17-1 Ote, Fukui, 910-8580, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Fukui Prefectural Government Office stands in for the administrative seat of the Seii nation in Episode 7, where rival generals negotiate and maneuver for political control of the northern kingdom.

ingredients.name: Kanazawa Castle
ingredients.address: Kanazawa Castle, 1-1 Marunouchi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kanazawa Castle is the fortress at the center of the Episode 7 'Kanazawa Night Raid,' where Muuton launches a bold but ultimately unsuccessful surprise assault against the Seii garrison, marking a pivotal turning point in the northern campaign.

ingredients.name: Asanogawa Bridge
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒920-0911 Ishikawa, Kanazawa, Hashibacho, 4 Asanogawa Bridge, 主計町－東山
ingredients.entityContext: Asanogawa Bridge in Kanazawa appears in Episode 7 during the night raid sequence, as forces cross the river under cover of darkness in one of the series' most tense infiltration scenes.

ingredients.name: Shibafune Koide Higashiyama Store
ingredients.address: Shibafune Koide Higashiyama Store, 1 Chome-13-13 Higashiyama, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-0831, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Higashiyama confectionery shop appears in Episode 7 as a background landmark along the old merchant quarter, lending the Kanazawa arc its distinctive historic-town atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Higashiyama Sugawara Shrine
ingredients.address: Higashiyama Sugawara Shrine, 1 Chome-27-7 Higashiyama, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-0831, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Higashiyama Sugawara Shrine is shown in Episode 7 as a quiet refuge in the Kanazawa Higashiyama district, visited by characters seeking respite amid the tensions of the Seii campaign.

ingredients.name: Maruoka Castle
ingredients.address: Maruoka Castle, 1 Chome-５９ Maruokacho Kasumicho, Sakai, Fukui 910-0231, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Maruoka Castle, one of Japan's oldest surviving castle keeps, appears in Episode 8 as a fortified stronghold Aoteru's forces must contend with on their advance into Fukui's interior.

ingredients.name: Morita Post Office
ingredients.entityContext: Morita Post Office in Fukui appears in Episode 8 as a local community landmark, used to depict the civilian life disrupted by the armies marching through Yamato's border territories.

ingredients.name: Mishima Taisha
ingredients.address: 2 Chome-1-5 Omiyacho, Mishima, Shizuoka 411-0035, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Mishima Taisha in Shizuoka appears in Episode 1 as a distant shrine referenced in backstory, distinct from the Iyo-Mishima area in Ehime that forms Aoteru's actual hometown setting.

ingredients.name: Daishiyama Seidai Temple
ingredients.address: Daishiyama Seidai Temple, 50-1-1 Katase, Katsuyama, Fukui 911-0811, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Daishiyama Seidai Temple in Katsuyama, Fukui appears in Episode 8, its towering Echizen Great Buddha providing a dramatic backdrop for a scene reflecting on sacrifice and resolve during the Seii campaign.

ingredients.name: Nakatofureai Park
ingredients.address: Nakatofureai Park, 8-28-8 Takayanagicho, Fukui, 910-0837, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Nakatofureai Park in Fukui appears in Episode 8 as a gathering point where local residents and soldiers briefly coexist, illustrating the human cost of the three-kingdoms conflict on ordinary communities.

ingredients.name: Fukui Prefectural Consumers' Cooperative Hearts Dinosaur
ingredients.address: Fukui Prefectural Dinosaur Museum, 51-11 Murokocho Terao, Katsuyama, Fukui 911-8601, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Fukui Prefectural Dinosaur Museum appears in Episode 6 as a recognizable local landmark, its distinctive silver dome visible as Aoteru's envoys travel through Katsuyama on their mission into Seii.

ingredients.name: Gohonjo Bridge
ingredients.address: Gobyo-bashi bridge, 550 Koyasan, Koya, Ito District, Wakayama 648-0211, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Gohonjo Bridge at Koyasan in Wakayama appears in the opening sequence, its ancient stone arches evoking the spiritual solemnity of the world Aoteru inherits and seeks to restore.

ingredients.name: Shinsekai
ingredients.address: Shinsekai, 2 Chome-5 Ebisuhigashi, Naniwa Ward, Osaka, 556-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shinsekai, the retro entertainment district beneath Tsutenkaku, appears in the opening and throughout the Osaka arc as the vibrant heart of the Yamato capital, where commoners and aspirants gather in the shadow of the tower that hosts the Toryumon exam.

ingredients.name: Colombo 1000 Mishima
ingredients.entityContext: Colombo 1000 Mishima in Shikokuchuo, Ehime appears in Episode 1 as a recognizable commercial landmark in Aoteru's hometown, grounding the story's opening in the everyday streetscape of the Seii borderlands.

ingredients.name: Gujo Observatory
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒799-0643 Ehime, Shikokuchuo, Sangawacho, Gujo Observatory, 乙254-46
ingredients.entityContext: Gujo Observatory in Ehime appears in Episode 1 with a sweeping coastal panorama, offering a vista over the Seto Inland Sea that underscores the isolation of Aoteru's hometown at the story's outset.

ingredients.name: Hiroshima Bank Mishima Branch
ingredients.localName: 広島銀行 三島支店
ingredients.address: Hiroshima Bank Mishima Branch, 1 Chome-7-34 Mishimachuo, Shikokuchuo, Ehime 799-0405, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Hiroshima Bank's Mishima branch in Shikokuchuo appears in Episode 1 as a background detail of the Ehime townscape, part of the series of local landmarks that establish Aoteru's origins in the former Seii borderlands.

ingredients.name: Osaka Castle
ingredients.address: Osaka Castle, 1-1 Osakajo, Chuo Ward, Osaka, 540-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Osaka Castle anchors the Yamato capital in Episode 1 and beyond, serving as the seat of power that Aoteru sets his sights on when he first arrives in the city to compete in the Toryumon examination.

ingredients.name: Harukiya Pharmacy
ingredients.address: Harukiya Pharmacy, 2 Chome-12-4 Mishimachuo, Shikokuchuo, Ehime 799-0405, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Harukiya Pharmacy in Shikokuchuo appears in Episode 1 as a detail of the Iyo-Mishima streetscape, one of the ordinary storefronts that populate Aoteru's hometown before he departs on his journey.

ingredients.name: Iyo-Mishima Station
ingredients.address: Iyo-Mishima Station, 3-chōme-1 Mishimachūō, Shikokuchuo, Ehime 799-0405, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Iyo-Mishima Station is the departure point in Episode 1 where Aoteru leaves his hometown behind, the platform scene marking the emotional beginning of his quest to reunify Japan.

ingredients.name: Naruto Mountain Lookout
ingredients.address: Naruto Mountain Lookout, Fukuike Narutocho Tosadomariura, Naruto, Tokushima 772-0053, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Naruto Mountain Lookout in Tokushima appears in Episode 1, its panoramic view over the Naruto Strait used to convey the fractured landscape of a Japan divided into warring kingdoms.

ingredients.name: IKEA Tsuruhama
ingredients.address: IKEA Tsuruhama, 2 Chome-24-55 Tsurumachi, Taishō-ku, Osaka, 551-0023, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: IKEA Tsuruhama in Osaka appears in Episode 2 as a background landmark in the Yamato capital's harbor district, part of the repurposed commercial infrastructure that survives in the post-collapse Osaka metropolis.

ingredients.name: Abeno Bridge
ingredients.address: Abeno Bridge, 5, Tennoji Ward, Osaka, 543-0063, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Abeno Bridge in Osaka appears in Episode 2 as a key crossing in the southern part of the Yamato capital, used during scenes of movement through the city as Aoteru navigates his early days in Osaka.

ingredients.name: Tsutenkaku
ingredients.address: Tsutenkaku, 1 Chome-18-6 Ebisuhigashi, Naniwa Ward, Osaka, 556-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tsutenkaku is the iconic tower that hosts the Toryumon, the high-stakes examination through which Aoteru enters the Yamato government's ranks in Episode 2. Its appearance signals the series' central theme of talent and ambition rising above birthright.

ingredients.name: Aiaubashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Aiaubashi Bridge, 1 Chome-1 Dotonbori, Chuo Ward, Osaka, 542-0071, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Aiaubashi Bridge near Dotonbori appears in Episode 4 during a dramatic confrontation sequence in Osaka's canal district, its romantic name and lively surroundings contrasting with the political tensions Aoteru faces.

ingredients.name: Kosekiya (Annex)
ingredients.address: Kosekiya (Annex), 417 銀山温泉 Obanazawa, Yamagata 999-4333, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Kosekiya Annex at Ginzan Onsen in Yamagata appears in Episode 4, the atmospheric Taisho-era inn standing in for a northern refuge where key characters hold a clandestine meeting away from the front lines.

ingredients.name: ニコタン･モモタン 新発田ガス(株)
ingredients.localName: ニコタン･モモタン 新発田ガス(株)
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒957-0127 Niigata, Kitakambara District, Seiro, Fujiyose, 3147-1 ニコタン･モモタン 新発田ガス(株)
ingredients.entityContext: This Niigata gas company location appears in Episode 4 as a background location during the Buo arc, its rural Niigata setting conveying the resource-scarce conditions that drive factional conflict in the northern kingdom.

ingredients.name: Shirakabe Barracks Public Relations Museum
ingredients.entityContext: The Shirakabe Barracks museum in Niigata appears in Episode 4, its historical military architecture repurposed in the story as a base of operations for Buo's regional forces during the inter-kingdom power struggle.

ingredients.name: Shibatajoshi Park Parking Lot
ingredients.address: Funaokajoshi Park Parking Lot, Tateyama Funaoka, Shibata, Shibata District, Miyagi 989-1606, Japan

ingredients.name: Shibata Castle Ruins
ingredients.address: Shibata Castle Ruins, 6 Chome-4-80 Otemachi, Shibata, Niigata 957-0052, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shibata Castle Ruins in Niigata appear in Episode 4 during the Buo arc, the crumbling fortification serving as a visual metaphor for the old order's collapse and the power vacuum that Aoteru must navigate.

ingredients.name: Ainokura Gassho-zukuri Village
ingredients.address: Ainokura Gassho-zukuri Village, Ainokura, Nanto, Toyama 939-1915, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Ainokura Gassho-zukuri Village in Toyama appears in Episode 4, its UNESCO-listed thatched farmhouses evoking the self-sufficient rural communities that survive on the margins of the three-kingdoms conflict.

ingredients.name: Sapporo Clock Tower
ingredients.address: Sapporo Clock Tower, 2 Chome Kita 1 Jonishi, Chuo Ward, Sapporo, Hokkaido 060-0001, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Sapporo Clock Tower appears in Episode 4 as a northern landmark, its Meiji-era architecture reflecting the anime's world where Japan has regressed to early-modern technology following societal collapse.

ingredients.name: Tsuzumi-mon Gate
ingredients.address: Tsuzumi-mon Gate, 2 Kinoshinbomachi, Kanazawa, Ishikawa 920-0858, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tsuzumi-mon Gate at Kanazawa Station appears in Episode 4 as the grand entrance to the Seii capital, its distinctive drum-shaped pillars making it one of the most recognizable settings in the show's Kanazawa arc.

ingredients.name: Temmabashi Bridge
ingredients.address: Temmabashi Bridge, 7 Tenmabashikyomachi, Chuo Ward, Osaka, 540-0032, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Temmabashi Bridge over the Okawa River in Osaka appears in Episode 5 during a pivotal strategy scene, the view of the old river-city skyline framing a key conversation between Aoteru and his Yamato allies.

ingredients.name: Tsutenkaku Hondori Shopping Street
ingredients.address: Tsutenkaku Hondori Shopping Street, 1 Chome-22-4 Ebisuhigashi, Naniwa Ward, Osaka, 556-0002, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Tsutenkaku Hondori Shopping Street appears in Episode 5 as the lively commercial artery beneath the tower, where Aoteru's supporters and ordinary citizens mingle in scenes that show the Yamato capital's resilient street culture.
